title: neisosperma brevitubum
intro: Neisosperma brevitubum, also known as the Pacific yew, is a plant species that belongs to the family Staphyleaceae. It is a small, deciduous tree native to Australia and New Caledonia, and its bark and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including anti-cancer and antimicrobial properties. Neisosperma brevitubum can be prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and its extracts have been used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
